mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income, boosting aggregate demand and expected corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.0% signals a tight labor market that generates wage-push inflation expectations, leading investors to price in accelerated monetary tightening that reduces equity valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A positive gap between the 3-month percent change in INDPRO and RSAFS indicates involuntary inventory accumulation that foreshadows an industrial slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The spread between INDPRO and RSAFS 3-month changes fails to produce a negative rank correlation (Rank IC > -0.03) with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, stronger retail sales data intensifies expectations of additional rate hikes, causing equity investors to de-rate multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS increased over the prior 3 months, positive surprises in RSAFS coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in industrial production signals accelerating manufacturing momentum that immediately boosts cyclical equity earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO exceeding 1.0% are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts signals strengthening residential investment and durable goods demand, which drives broad economic expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST fail to associate with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (Rank IC < 0.03) during expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating consumer price index signals rising inflation expectations that increase the equity risk premium and compress valuation multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Month-over-month acceleration in CPIAUCSL is followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals rapid labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in UNRATE exceeding 0.2pp are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences.
